rationale: Falsifiability/AC-quality lens, round 2. Ask 1 (populated round-trip) ADDRESSED: new AC7 TestLoad_PopulatedConfigRoundTrips loads a five-phase models: map, custom 2+2 reviewers with approve_threshold "3", a full valid loop: block, and runner: claude-code-workflow and asserts every field round-trips unchanged — closes R3's untested populated half and supplies the non-empty-input proofs R2/R4 lacked; same anchored grep-PASS pattern, no false-pass. Ask 2 (ADR content anchors) PARTIAL-but-substantial: new AC8 promotes the greps into the checklist and makes 5 of R1's 6 falsification sub-clauses runnable (Accepted, four layer labels L1-L4, 'never casually up', 'artifact + CLI contract'); the sole residual is R1's 'omits the precedent chain' clause, which still has no grep anchor. That is a one-line add (grep 'spec 102'/'ADR-0037'/'ADR-0034') and is verified by Bead 1's own ADR panel, so it is advisory, not gate-blocking. Rebuilt matrix for the new text: reviewer floor (count>=1 AND sum>=2) both in AC3; third-defense pin (threshold>0, resolved-0->Block) in AC5; handoff_log default in AC1 + round-trip in AC7. Every falsification clause except the precedent-chain narrative now has a covering AC. Approve.
findings: ASK 1 (R5.1 populated round-trip) — ADDRESSED. AC7 (TestLoad_PopulatedConfigRoundTrips) loads a populated models: map (all five phases), custom reviewers (2+2, approve_threshold "3"), a full valid loop: block, and runner: claude-code-workflow, asserting field-by-field round-trip. This is exactly the non-empty-input proof R3 falsification ('populated models: map does not round-trip') required, plus the R2 (list-not-dropped) and R4 (loop parse) round-trip proofs my round-1 verdict asked for as extras. Anchored -run '...$' + grep '--- PASS' — robust, no false-pass.

ASK 2 (R5.2 ADR-0040 content anchors in AC checklist) — PARTIAL. AC8 promotes the grep set into the AC checklist and anchors 5 of R1's 6 falsification sub-clauses: file-present, Status: Accepted, four layer labels (L1/L2/L3/L4), ratchet direction ('never casually up'), portability ('artifact + CLI contract'). RESIDUAL: R1 still lists 'omits the precedent chain' as a falsification, but no runnable grep covers it. My round-1 ask named precedent chain explicitly, so I mark this PARTIAL — not MISSED (the bulk landed) and not gate-blocking.

ADVISORY (residual of Ask 2): add one anchor to AC8 for the precedent chain, e.g. `grep -q 'spec 102' "$F"` or `grep -q 'ADR-0037' "$F"` / `grep -q 'ADR-0034' "$F"` (the spec 102 in-binary-gate + ADR-0037 mechanized matrix + ADR-0034 ceremony-collapse chain R1 requires the ADR to cite). Cheap, additive at Bead-1 authoring time; without it the precedent-chain clause relies on the ADR panel read rather than a runnable falsifier — the same acceptable-but-soft posture as R7's 'no second interpreter' and R9's file-mutation clauses.

NEW-TEXT MATRIX (reviewer floor, third-defense pin, handoff_log): all covered. Reviewer floor (R5: reviewers[].count>=1 AND sum(reviewers.count)>=2) — BOTH sub-clauses enumerated in AC3 ('a reviewers[].count below 1, and a reviewers set summing below 2 each make Load return an error'). Third-defense pin (R8: gate.go threshold>0 guard; falsified if a resolved-0-threshold panel ever returns Allow) — AC5 asserts ExpectedReviewers=1/absent-field (resolved threshold 0) still returns Block, pinning the guard behaviorally. handoff_log (R4: default AUTOPILOT-LOG.md) — asserted in AC1 defaults and round-tripped via AC7's full valid loop block.

R<->AC full recheck: R2 (AC1 defaults + AC7 custom-reviewers round-trip proves list not dropped), R3 (AC1 empty default + AC7 populated map — NOW COVERED, was the round-1 gap), R4 (AC1 all defaults incl handoff_log + AC7 loop parse), R5 (AC3 enumerates all 9 refusal inputs incl both floors), R6 (AC2 sum + raw-unresolved), R7 (AC4 absent/n-1/in-range/out-of-range incl recorded-0-never-0), R8 (AC5 decision-invariance + note empty/non-empty + resolved-0-Block; import ban via go list -deps in Validation Proofs), R9 (AC6 omits-key + marks-inert), R10 (AC1 default + AC3 unknown-runner-errors + AC6 surfaced). Only uncovered falsification clause repo-wide: R1's precedent chain (above).

SOFT (unchanged from round 1, acceptable): R7 'no second interpreter is introduced anywhere' and R9 'mutates any file'/'exits non-zero' remain code-review / Validation-Proof invariants rather than AC-checkable; their behavioral halves ARE in AC4/AC6. Consistent posture with the precedent-chain residual.
POSITIVE: the grep-PASS-line AC pattern remains false-pass-proof on the two new ACs; AC8's `&&`-chained grep exits non-zero if ANY anchor is missing, so it fails closed on partial content.
